notes:
## ChatOSS 0.5.0 — the app platform release

ChatOSS is now a real OS for apps: you can build, install, and share your own apps as portable `.aip` files.

### Apps (.aip)
- **Install real third-party apps** — a `.aip` is just a zip of plain HTML/CSS/JS with an `app.json` manifest. Drop it on the Apps app and it lands in your dock with its own icon.
- Apps run **sandboxed** and talk to the OS only through `window.chatoss`: private + shared storage, streaming AI chat on your selected model (no API keys in apps), AI tool calling, native file dialogs, drag-and-drop, and approval-gated terminal access.
- App files are served at real URLs via the new `aip://` protocol — relative scripts, images, and `fetch()` just work.
- Apps can publish AI tools that ChatOSS's own agents can call while the app is open.

### Security & permissions
- Every sensitive capability is declared in the manifest and approved by you, with the app's own "what" and "why" shown in the prompt.
- Terminal approval is **per program** — allowing `git` never silently covers anything else.
- A **Permissions** panel on every installed app shows each decision, with one-click reset.
- Updating an app that requests **new** permissions shows you the exact additions first — deny keeps your current version.

### Dock redesign
- Colorful app-tile icons for the working apps; installed apps sit next to them as equals.
- The **active app shows its name**; everything else is icon-only with hover names.
- Utilities (Apps, Connect, Docs) are pinned on the right, and the dock adapts to narrow windows.

### Docs app overhaul
- Three complete, **installable example apps** — Notepad (basics), To-Do (AI tools), Chatbot (streaming chat) — each with a clickable file tree.
- A full `window.chatoss` API reference, a 5-minute quickstart, and honest permission docs.
- **Copy page** on every page (markdown for AI conversations) and a **Build with AI** guide you paste to any AI agent so it can build `.aip` apps for you.
